blocks.content: A desperate cry echoed through the Great World War.
Arius, shot by electricity, flew 10 meters and rolled on the ground.
The magic Apoptosis, which dives into one's thoughts, trapped him in an eternal concept where time did not exist.
It was a kind of mental death, and the shock upon returning was incomparable to the fear of a newborn baby.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Aaah! Aaah!

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Everything was confusing and unsettling.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Only the faint remnants of memories vaguely told him who he was.
At the boundary of that oblivion, a thin thread of intelligence flowed in and connected with his memories.
The scene of casting magic to avoid Captain Walker's sword in the Kazra Palace came to mind, and the screams subsided.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Damn, I can't keep doing this.
Wiping the drool from his chin, Arius stood up, feeling suspicious of the unfamiliar surroundings.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Where is this...?
If someone had pulled him out of Apoptosis, it would be one of the Seven Magical Sins, and it should be their secret hideout, but the scenery was entirely different.
Everything was in a light purple metallic color, with bizarre machinery emitting light and operating.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Are you the grave robber?
Arius turned around with an unpleasant expression.
But he had to look up, and his pupils contracted in surprise.
A halo above the head, wings of light several times larger than the body.
An angel.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Why is an angel here...?
As the question filled his mind, he quickly suppressed it with the Flicker magic battery.
He had to escape.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Thinking came next.
Just as his body turned into light with a flash, Uriel's hand moved faster than a moment.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Ugh!
Arius, grabbed by the neck, struggled three meters above the ground.
It felt like his neck bone was being held, not just his neck.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: S-save me...!
When Uriel slammed Arius to the ground, he arched his back in pain and groaned.
Then, struggling, he knelt on one knee and looked up at the angels.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Damn, who are you?

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: I am Uriel, the Angel of Destruction.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: U-Uriel?
One of the eight archangels in Heaven.
Arius quickly scanned his surroundings.
He recognized the objects, devices, and the galaxy mirror, realizing it was the Great World War.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Then the one next to him is Kariel...
With two archangels present, even Arius of the Seven Magical Sins had no chance of leaving this place alive on his own.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: I have something to show you.
Kariel, who had lifted Arius into the air, flew to the galaxy mirror and threw him again.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Ugh!
Falling from his solar plexus, he gasped for breath and then realized someone was in front of him, struggling to lift his head.
A woman was sitting cross-legged.

blocks.speaker: Uriel
blocks.content: A human?

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Adrias Miro.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: M-Miro? This woman?
To a scale magician, Miro was an unforgettable name.
But even the grave robber Arius had never seen her face before.
So this is what she looks like.
An ordinary girl you could see anywhere in the world. The kind that might catch your eye for a moment if you passed her on the street.
But the adjectives attached to her were all superlatives: best, last, ultimate, strongest.
So Arius couldn't help but stare at the young girl's face, mesmerized.
Beautiful, just by existing.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Quickly regaining his senses, Arius analyzed the situation based on existing information.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Archangels, Great World War, Miro.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Damn it. So it's come to this?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Miro being in Heaven could be interpreted as the final war being imminent.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: What the hell is going on? Do they know in the original world?
He wanted to talk to Miro, but she showed no signs of waking up.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Did you put her to sleep?

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: No. Miro is in the dimension of emptiness. You can bring her back to her original state.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Dimension of emptiness...

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Damn! It's Samadhi.
Now he was starting to get the picture.
As long as Miro was in Samadhi, she couldn't be killed. And that was why they had summoned him, the grave robber.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Destroy Miro's dimension of emptiness.
Arius snorted.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: When Uriel had grabbed him, he instinctively begged for his life, but now that he had regained his intellect, death was not a priority.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: If he pulled Miro out of the dimension of emptiness, her spacetime would be destroyed, and humanity would face extinction.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: I don't really care, but I don't like the idea of doing what these guys want.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Still, he had no intention of giving up.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: He was about to explore the mind of one of the greatest magicians in human history.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: What she liked, what she hated, what made her feel shame, and what secrets she would take to her grave.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Destroy Samadhi?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Are you kidding me?

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: If you can't, you will die.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: You don't think that's a threat, do you? This woman is the strongest scale magician in humanity. Anyone who dives in will be crushed by the egoist.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Then you will die.
When Kariel extended his hand, Arius raised both hands as if to calm him down.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Wait. I didn't say I wouldn't do it. I'm just saying we need to find a way around it.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: A way around? Her mind?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: If I dive in now, I'll be swept away by Samadhi.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: But what about Drimo? In Drimo, where any thought flows, I can collect Miro's mind.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: There, I can find a strong enough keyword to break Samadhi.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Keyword?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Every human has trauma. Trauma so severe that just thinking about it stops their breath.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: We create a powerful net with those and throw it into the abyss. Then when we pull it, we catch the big fish, Miro.
Kariel stared at Arius, who was excitedly explaining.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Is he sane? How can he be so enthusiastic about destroying humanity?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: I can't understand humans at all.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: But Drimo is a multidimensional structure, not a place you can investigate at will.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: So give me some information. You must know something.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Hmm.
Suddenly, Kariel's halo expanded into a ring.
As Arius's face turned pale, a black sphere emerged, tearing through space.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Come forth, Brahma.
Kiiiii!
A hooked nose appeared first, followed by a red-skinned face.
The eyes were slanted unpleasantly, and the teeth were irregular.
Three glowing triangles floated on the forehead, and the brain extended beyond the crown, flowing like hair down to the small figure's feet.
Kariel's familiar, the three-horned Mara Brahma.

blocks.speaker: Brahma
blocks.content: Lowly Brahma greets Archangel Kariel.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Follow this human to Drimo. Assist him in whatever he investigates.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Damn, Brahma?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: It was shocking for a magician to see the god of intelligence from mythology looking like that.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: But he quickly regained his composure.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: This was an opportunity.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: With the knowledge of the three-horned Mara, he could access the deepest parts of Drimo, where humanity had never reached.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Hehe, the god of magic is smiling upon me.
Arius approached Miro.
He would uncover everything about her.
And when he returned, he would be the god of the world.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Let's go. Heaven seems quite busy.
Brahma extended a strand of his brain through Miro's mouth into her brain.
As the brain area governing Drimo activated, a small spacetime tunnel appeared before him.
Arius leaped in without hesitation, followed by Brahma.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Whew.
Only after the spacetime tunnel closed did Kariel sit down.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Summoning the three-horned Mara had drained all my remaining power.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Are you alright?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: My strength will return with time. Once Miro wakes up, humanity is finished. I'll have the right to initiate at the archangel council.
It might not succeed. Brahma is not in a normal state due to the law of familiars.
This follows the principle that no acquired concept can appear without a preceding concept.
This is the law of familiars.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: It doesn't matter. Brahma was born by mimicking my intellect. He can make decisions in a second that would take ten thousand cautious humans to reach a compromise. It will take time, but he can do it.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: It would take time. But what did it matter?
After all, it was the last time humanity had left.
The strategy room was engulfed in silence.
Niflheim.
A place even the mainland's border guards were forbidden to approach, inhabited only by evil spirits that once terrified humans.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Let me be clear, it's an extremely dangerous place. Considering the value of Black Elixir, many have tried, but no one has set foot there for thousands of years. The reason is simple. If you go, you die. So choose wisely.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Do you think we have a choice in this situation?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Even if you don't go to Niflheim, we can maintain our alliance. The opportunity isn't just now. But for the 1st Headquarters to engage in full-scale war, these three conditions must be met.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Gaold, what will you do?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: We're going to Niflheim.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Hmm.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: I hold you in high regard. That's why I want to dissuade you. Using the light of Sector 73 to expand the rebel forces is also a good strategy. Why are you in such a hurry? Is there a special reason?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Haste leads to mistakes.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Gaold knew this too, which is why he was desperately trying to stay calm.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: But objectively, time was not on their side.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: They couldn't kill Miro in Samadhi, but they wouldn't be doing this without a plan either.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: You don't need to know that. We'll meet the three conditions as quickly as possible. Is that satisfactory?

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Of course. If it's possible.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Now we'll split into teams. Kangnan, Zulu, and I will go to Niflheim.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: What about the rest?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Armin, Siina, Etella, and Kuan will handle the military community. Shirone and Plu will persuade the 2nd Headquarters. This is the only way.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Then I'll stay here and work on the strategy. I also need to understand planetary movements.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: To accurately strike Aravot with divine retribution, precise predictions of the landing point were necessary. Understanding planetary movements was a prerequisite.

blocks.speaker: Crude
blocks.content: Are you really going to Niflheim?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: No.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: No.
